basic_details.job_title: SSC JHT Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 84 Junior Hindi Translator Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Staff Selection Commission (SSC), Government of India
basic_details.post_name: Junior Hindi Translator (JHT), Junior Translation Officer (JTO), Junior Translator (JT), Senior Hindi Translator (SHT), Senior Translation Officer (STO)
basic_details.short_information: Staff Selection Commission (SSC) has released the official Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 notification on 23 April 2026 at its official website https://ssc.gov.in. A total of 84 tentative vacancies have been announced for the posts of Junior Hindi Translator, Junior Translation Officer, Junior Translator, Senior Hindi Translator and Senior Translation Officer under various Ministries/Departments/Organizations of the Government of India. Eligible candidates aged between 18 to 30 years with a Master's degree in Hindi or English along with requisite translation diploma or experience

application_fee.details: The application fee for SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 is ₹100 for General (UR), OBC, and EWS male candidates. Women candidates and candidates belonging to Scheduled Castes (SC), Scheduled Tribes (ST), Persons with Benchmark Disabilities (PwBD), and Ex-Servicemen eligible for reservation are fully exempted from payment of fee. | Fee can be paid online only through BHIM UPI, Net Banking, or by using Visa, MasterCard, Maestro, or RuPay Debit Card. The fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances and will not be adjusted against any other examination. Candidates must ensure their fee payment is complete - if the fee is not received by SSC, the application status will show as "Incomplete" and will be summarily rejected.

age_limit.details: The age limit for SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 is between 18 and 30 years, calculated as on the crucial date of 01.08.2026. Candidates must have been born not before 02.08.1996 and not later than 01.08.2008 to be eligible. Age relaxation is applicable to candidates belonging to reserved categories as per Government of India rules.

qualification.education_qualification: Master's degree in Hindi/English with translation diploma or experience
qualification.details: Nationality: Citizen of India or eligible subjects of Nepal/Bhutan or persons of Indian origin from specified countries with a Government of India eligibility certificate. | Educational Qualification

selection_process: Stage Details
1. Paper-I (CBE) Objective type; used for shortlisting category-wise candidates for Paper-II. Minimum qualifying marks: UR 30%, OBC/EWS 25%, Others 20%
2. Paper-II (Descriptive) Translation passages (Hindi ↔ English) and Essay; 200 marks; qualifying based on Paper-I merit
3. Final Merit 50% weightage of Paper-I + 100% weightage of Paper-II marks
4. Post Preference & Allocation Online Option Form to be submitted before FRTA; merit-cum-preference based allocation
5. Document Verification Conducted by User Departments/Organizations after declaration of final result
1st Higher total marks in Paper-II
2nd Date of Birth - older candidate preferred
3rd Alphabetical order of names of candidates
The selection process for SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 consists of two stages. In the first stage
Paper-I (Computer Based Examination) is used to shortlist candidates category-wise for Paper-II. Minimum qualifying marks in Paper-I are 30% for UR
25% for OBC/EWS
and 20% for all other categories. In the second stage
Paper-II (Descriptive) is conducted for shortlisted candidates.
For the final merit list

50% weightage is given to Paper-I scores and 100% weightage to Paper-II marks. Final selection and allocation of Ministry/Department/Organization is based on merit-cum-preference of posts/departments exercised by candidates through an Online Option Form. In case of a tie
it is resolved by total marks in Paper-II
then date of birth (older preferred)
and finally alphabetical order of names. Selected candidates will be on probation for 2 years and all posts carry All India Service Liability (AISL).
Tie-Breaking Order
how_to_apply: The application process for SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 is entirely online at https://ssc.gov.in. Candidates who have not yet completed their One-Time Registration (OTR) on the new SSC website must do so first, as the earlier OTR from the old website (ssc.nic.in) is not valid for the new portal. Aadhaar-based biometric authentication is available and recommended - candidates who opt for it will not face rejection on grounds of photograph or signature quality.

The application module is designed to capture a live photograph of the candidate through webcam or mobile device; pre-existing photographs must not be submitted. Candidates must also upload their scanned signature in JPEG/JPG format (10-20 KB). Applications with blurred or miniature photographs or signatures will be summarily rejected unless Aadhaar authentication has been opted for. Only one online application per candidate is permitted during the normal application period.
Visit the official website https://ssc.gov.in and complete One-Time Registration (OTR).
Login using Registration Number and Password.
Click on "Apply" under "Combined Hindi Translators Examination, 2026" in the Live Exam tab.
Fill in educational details, additional information (ESM/age relaxation), and examination centre preferences (up to 3 centres).
Capture live photograph through webcam/mobile as per instructions (no cap, mask, or spectacles).
Upload scanned signature in JPEG/JPG format (10-20 KB), dimensions ~6.0 cm (W) × 2.0 cm (H).
PwBD/PwD candidates must upload Disability Certificate as per Annexure-I/IA/X/XI, whichever is applicable.
Preview the application carefully, complete the declaration, and submit before 14.05.2026 (23:00 hours).
Pay the application fee (if applicable) online through BHIM UPI, Net Banking, or Debit Card on or before 15.05.2026 (23:00 hours).
Take a printout of the submitted application for future reference.
important_instructions: Candidates applying for SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 must read the official notification carefully in its entirety before filling and submitting the application form. The English version of the notice will prevail in case of any dispute. Candidates are advised to submit their applications well before the closing date to avoid last-minute server load issues on the SSC website. All candidatures are provisional - the Commission does not undertake detailed scrutiny at the time of the written examination, and eligibility will be verified only at the Document Verification stage by the User Departments. Any suppression or misrepresentation of information in the application can lead to cancellation of candidature at any stage, including after selection. Canvassing in any form will result in immediate disqualification. The decision of the Commission in all matters including eligibility, selection, and allocation of posts is final and binding.

Read the official notice carefully before applying; English version prevails in case of dispute. Only one online application is permitted per candidate; multiple applications will be rejected. Name, DOB, father's name, and mother's name must match the Matriculation Certificate exactly. Candidates must keep a printout of their application for at least 3 years for future reference. Post preference must be submitted online before FRTA declaration; options once submitted are final. All posts carry All India Service Liability (AISL) - candidates may be posted anywhere in India. Regularly check https://ssc.gov.in for updates on exam dates, vacancies, admit cards, and results.

faq.question: Q1. What is SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026?
faq.answer: SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 is a national‑level recruitment exam conducted by the Staff Selection Commission (SSC) for Group ‘B’ Non‑Gazetted posts like Junior Hindi Translator, Junior Translation Officer, Junior Translator, Senior Hindi Translator and Senior Translation Officer in various Central Government Ministries, Departments and Organizations.

faq.question: Q2. What are the important dates for SSC Hindi Translator 2026?
faq.answer: The SSC CHT 2026 notification was released on 23 April 2026 and the online application window is open from 23.04.2026 to 14.05.2026 (up to 23:00 hours). The correction window will remain open from 19.05.2026 to 20.05.2026 and Paper‑I (Computer Based Examination) is scheduled in August–September 2026.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are there in SSC Combined Hindi Translators 2026?
faq.answer: SSC has notified a total of 84 tentative vacancies for the Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026 across different post codes and departments. These include posts in Central Secretariat Official Language Service (CSOLS), Armed Forces Headquarters (AFHQ) and various other Ministries/Departments/Organizations.

faq.question: Q4. What posts are covered under SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026?
faq.answer: The examination covers the posts of Junior Hindi Translator (JHT), Junior Translation Officer (JTO), Junior Translator (JT), Senior Hindi Translator (SHT), Senior Translator (ST) and Senior Translation Officer (STO). Junior‑level posts generally fall in Pay Level‑6 (₹35,400–1,12,400), while Senior‑level posts are in Pay Level‑7 (₹44,900–1,42,400).

faq.question: Q5. What is the eligibility and educational qualification for SSC JHT 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have a Master’s degree in Hindi with English as a compulsory/elective subject at graduation level, or a Master’s degree in English with Hindi as a compulsory/elective subject, or a Master’s in any subject with Hindi/English as medium or compulsory subject at degree level. In addition, they must possess either a recognized Diploma/Certificate in translation (Hindi–English–Hindi) or relevant translation experience (2 years for junior posts and 3 years for senior posts) in Central/State Government or PSU offices.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for SSC Combined Hindi Translators Exam 2026?
faq.answer: The age limit is 18 to 30 years as on 01.08.2026 for general category candidates. Permissible relaxations include 5 years for SC/ST, 3 years for OBC (Non‑Creamy Layer), and up to 10–15 years for PwBD candidates depending on their category, as per Government of India rules.

faq.question: Q7. What is the application fee for SSC Hindi Translator 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is ₹100 for General (UR), OBC and EWS male candidates, payable online through BHIM UPI, Net Banking or debit/credit cards. Women candidates and candidates belonging to SC, ST, PwBD and eligible Ex‑Servicemen categories are exempted from payment of examination fee.

faq.question: Q8. What is the exam pattern and marking scheme for SSC JHT 2026?
faq.answer: SSC JHT 2026 consists of two papers – Paper‑I (Computer Based Examination) and Paper‑II (Descriptive). Paper‑I has 100 questions each in General Hindi and General English (200 marks, 2 hours, 0.25 negative marking per wrong answer, with sectional timing), while Paper‑II is a descriptive test of translation and essay writing in Hindi and English for 200 marks.

faq.question: Q9. How is the final merit prepared in SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates are first shortlisted for Paper‑II based on their marks in Paper‑I, subject to minimum qualifying marks (30% for UR, 25% for OBC/EWS and 20% for other categories). Final merit is then prepared by giving 50% weightage to Paper‑I and 100% weightage to Paper‑II, followed by document verification and allocation of posts based on merit‑cum‑preference.

faq.question: Q10. How can I apply online for SSC Combined Hindi Translators Examination 2026?
faq.answer: You must first complete One‑Time Registration (OTR) on the new SSC website (https://ssc.gov.in) and then log in with your registration credentials. After login, select “Combined Hindi Translators Examination, 2026” from the Live Exams section, fill the online form, capture live photograph, upload signature and required documents, pay the fee (if applicable) and submit the application before 14 May 2026.
